title: pyrus × intermedia
intro: Pyrus × intermedia, also known as the hawthorn tree, is a cross between the whitebeam (Sorbus aria) and the wild service tree (Sorbus torminalis), but in many references it is also referred to as a cross between the European pear (Pyrus communis) and the oriental pear (Pyrus pyrifolia). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating heart and circulatory issues, due to its high levels of flavonoids and anthocyanins. The health benefits of Pyrus × intermedia include reducing inflammation, improving blood pressure, and enhancing cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as tea and capsules, are commonly used to support heart health and improve overall well-being.
